description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a flat roof and a prominent front porch supported by white columns and detailed railings. The exterior is clad in light gray horizontal siding with white trim around the windows and roofline, and the foundation is finished with red brick. The front facade features three large, symmetrically arranged windows on the second floor, each with decorative muntins, and a central entry door accessed by a short set of brick steps. Two red brick chimneys rise from the roof, and the rear of the house includes a small back porch with a checkered tile floor. The side elevations are relatively plain, with evenly spaced windows and minimal ornamentation. The overall design suggests a blend of Colonial Revival and American Foursquare architectural influences, common in early 20th-century urban neighborhoods.
description: A two-story gray house with a flat roof, front porch, white trim, and red brick foundation, featuring symmetrical windows and decorative details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry with porch, steps, three upper windows, and decorative roof trim.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the long, plain siding, two chimneys, and minimal window arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view with a small back porch, checkered tile floor, and simple window placement.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with plain siding and evenly spaced windows.
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with brick steps, covered porch, white columns, and decorative railing
